text:
User: ok, wait, no bro, this needs to be cleaned up -- there's zero reason why we would have diferent logic for creating session channels or non-session channels, it's literally the exact same thing, the only difference is where does the name come from, like either autogenerated or not, but this is literally the same logic!

Assistant: You're absolutely right. Let me read both functions in full to unify them into one shared subgroup-creation path. [...] `ensure_channel_ready` is now the single channel-provisioning path. Both `ensure_session_room` and `rpc_channels_create` are thin wrappers over it — they just supply the name (auto-generated vs operator-chosen) and the member, exactly as you said. The duplicated create+lock+admin-poll+copy-admins-down blocks are gone (`server.rs`: −342 net lines).

- `rpc_channels_create`: stamp name → `ensure_channel_ready(child, parent_hint=parent, member=creator)` → publish orchestration → return. Parent now always ensured (recursively).
- `ensure_session_room`: mark session-room marker → `ensure_channel_ready(room, parent_hint=parent, member=agent)` → rename to title. 
- Strengthened the shared primitive with the admin-reflection poll the duplicated paths had (so a freshly-created group's dependent grants apply first-try — nothing regressed).
